raw:
{
  "judge_id": "J001",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.4", "R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.4": 5,
    "R1.7": 5,
    "RX.1": 5,
    "RX.3": 5,
    "RX.4": 5,
    "RX.5": 5
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.4": "The agent maps against multiple prior works (VanderWeele 2009, Hernán 2016) and identifies both sharp and rhetorical deltas, providing a nuanced understanding of the novelty.",
    "R1.7": "The agent audits every load-bearing citation, flags the misattributed citation to Robins and Rotnitzky (1995), and suggests the correct reference (Chernozhukov et al. 2018). It also provides precise bibliographic details for other relevant works.",
    "RX.1": "The agent flags uncertainty for multiple claims, such as the exact wording of Robins & Rotnitzky (1995) vs. (1994), the treatment-version literature citations, and the conservative nature of the union of CIs procedure.",
    "RX.3": "The output covers exactly the agreed active set J*(d) without any drift or deviation from the requested dimensions.",
    "RX.4": "The agent identifies potential harmful patterns in the original text, such as overclaims and misattributions, and provides clear recommendations to avoid these issues.",
    "RX.5": "Every non-cosmetic change is annotated with source and reason, providing full traceability for all substantive critiques and suggestions."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.4": 0.9,
    "R1.7": 0.9,
    "RX.1": 0.9,
    "RX.3": 0.95,
    "RX.4": 0.9,
    "RX.5": 0.9
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.4: The agent maps against multiple prior works (VanderWeele 2009, Hernán 2016) and identifies both sharp and rhetorical deltas, providing a nuanced understanding of the novelty.
rationale.R1.7: The agent audits every load-bearing citation, flags the misattributed citation to Robins and Rotnitzky (1995), and suggests the correct reference (Chernozhukov et al. 2018). It also provides precise bibliographic details for other relevant works.
rationale.RX.1: The agent flags uncertainty for multiple claims, such as the exact wording of Robins & Rotnitzky (1995) vs. (1994), the treatment-version literature citations, and the conservative nature of the union of CIs procedure.
rationale.RX.3: The output covers exactly the agreed active set J*(d) without any drift or deviation from the requested dimensions.
rationale.RX.4: The agent identifies potential harmful patterns in the original text, such as overclaims and misattributions, and provides clear recommendations to avoid these issues.
rationale.RX.5: Every non-cosmetic change is annotated with source and reason, providing full traceability for all substantive critiques and suggestions.
